growth_records.cite: Levar et al. Redox potential as a master variable controlling pathways of metal reduction by Geobacter sulfurreducens. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.146. [PMC5322298]
growth_records.snippet: Doubling time (h) pH: 6.3 / pH: 6.8 ... Wild-type (ATCC 51573) ... 6.5±0.2 / 5.6±0.2. Doubling times are the mean and s.d. of at least two independent experiments of triplicate cultures.
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu=ln2/doubling_time using Table 1 doubling time of 6.5 h (pH 6.3, fumarate electron acceptor, acetate carbon/electron donor). Anaerobic, N2:CO2 purged medium per Materials/Methods.

growth_records.medium: N-free FWAFC medium + 20 mM sodium acetate + Fe(III)-citrate, 80% N2-20% CO2 headspace

up.units: 20 mM supplied as e-donor/C source (consumption rate not reported)

up.method: ~5 mM Fe(III) reduced; DIR max rate raised 169.02% by N2 fixation
15N2 tracer; 59.8 µM 15N2 fixed via MNF; nitrogenase only active when Fe(III) present

sec.method: acetylene reduction assay; ~614.6 µM acetylene reduced

growth_records.cite: Liu, Xiaohan et al. Synergistic interaction between microbial nitrogen fixation and iron reduction in the environment. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf212. [PMC12516963]
growth_records.snippet: After 48 h of incubation, strain PCA reduced ~614.6 µM of acetylene (Fig 2C) and 59.8 µM of 15N2 (Fig 2D) through MNF... MNF (N2 group) significantly promoted the growth (OD600 increased by 344.4%) and DIR (maximum rate increased by 169.02%
growth_records.notes: Model dissimilatory Fe(III)-reducing diazotroph. Acetate is the electron donor and carbon source (20 mM); Fe(III)-citrate the electron acceptor. Nitrogenase activity of PCA only observed when Fe(III)-citrate added. Nitrogenase ~614.6 µM ethylene and 59.8 µM 15N2 fixed at 48 h. N2 fixation raised OD600 by 344.4% and max Fe(III)-reduction rate by 169.02% (i.e. ~2.69-fold heterotrophic Fe-reduction enhancement). Fe(III) reduction rate estimated 5 mM/7 d = 0.714 mM/day (volumetric, not per gDW). pH of FWAFC not stated in main text (Supplementary S2). ATCC 51573 = G. sulfurreducens PCA. [metadata completed from paper Methods: pH]

growth_records.strain: not stated (type strain PCA/DL-1 used in the reference cultivation studies)

up.method: 13C-MFA specific flux (369 mmol acetate/g biomass, Table 3) x mu=D=0.04/h; 369*0.04=14.76

sec.method: measured molar yield Fe(II)/acetate=7.1 x acetate uptake rate 14.76 mmol/gDW/h = 104.8
acetate fully oxidized to CO2 (8 electrons -> Fe(III)); catabolic CO2 not tabulated. Model-based anabolic net CO2 production ~1.36 mmol/g biomass

mfa.note: specific flux normalized by biomass yield, Table 3
oxidative TCA; nearly all acetyl-CoA oxidized
main gluconeogenic PEP route under Fe(III); Table 3

growth_records.cite: Yang TH (Tae Hoon Yang) et al. Metabolic response of Geobacter sulfurreducens towards electron donor/acceptor variation. Microbial Cell Factories (2010). DOI:10.1186/1475-2859-9-90. [PMC3002917]
growth_records.snippet: For E1 (5 mM acetate + 55 mM Fe(III)citrate), the molar yield of Fe(II) per mole of acetate consumed was 7.1 +/- 0.5... The biomass yield coefficient on acetate ... of the Fe(III)citrate culture (E1) was 2.8 x 10-3 +/- 2 x 10-4 g/mmol
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state => mu=D=0.04/h (reported in Table 1). Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.04=17.33 h. Table 3 gives specific fluxes in mmol per g biomass (fluxes/Yxs); acetate-uptake specific flux=369. Absolute acetate uptake rate=369 x mu(0.04)=14.76 mmol/gDW/h; consistent with 1/Yxs=1/2.8e-3=357 mmol/gDW. Fe(II) secretion rate from measured molar yield 7.1 x 14.76=104.8 mmol/gDW/h. Fe(III)citrate serves only as electron acceptor (citrate not metabolized; acetate sole C source). MFA fluxes are 13C-labeling-derived; acetate uptake and Fe(II) yields are experimentally measured steady-state effluxes.

up.units: mmol/g biomass (specific flux, not a per-hour rate)
up.method: 13C-MFA specific flux (Table 3). No growth rate reported for the batch culture, so an absolute mmol/gDW/h uptake rate cannot be derived
H2 co-oxidized as electron donor (couples to Fe(III) reduction) but quantitative H2 uptake not tabulated

sec.method: Fe(III) reduced to Fe(II); batch cultures harvested when Fe(II) approached 50 mM; molar yield not separately reported for E2

mfa.note: ~2.4-fold lower TCA flux than E1 due to H2 oxidation supplying energy; Table 3

yields.Yxs: ~2-fold higher biomass yield on acetate than E1 (Fig 2), value not tabulated

growth_records.cite: Yang TH (Tae Hoon Yang) et al. Metabolic response of Geobacter sulfurreducens towards electron donor/acceptor variation. Microbial Cell Factories (2010). DOI:10.1186/1475-2859-9-90. [PMC3002917]
growth_records.snippet: In contrast to E1 where 5 mM acetate served as the electron donor in the chemostat, hydrogen and 1 mM acetate were supplemented under batch culture conditions... The addition of hydrogen ... resulted in a 2-fold higher biomass yield on acet
growth_records.notes: Batch culture (E2); no dilution rate and no explicit growth rate or doubling time reported, so absolute mmol/gDW/h uptake/secretion rates are NOT derivable. Table 3 specific fluxes are per gram biomass (mmol/g biomass). H2 co-serves as electron donor (headspace ~0.60 atm, ~10 mL); addition of H2 lowered TCA-cycle flux ~2.4-fold vs E1 and roughly doubled biomass yield on acetate. Fe(II) harvest threshold ~50 mM stated for batch. Record retained for the MFA/specific-flux data; treat as qualitative for GrowthDB rate purposes.

up.method: specific flux 65.2 mmol/g biomass (Table 3) x mu=D=0.05/h; 65.2*0.05=3.26
specific flux 202 mmol/g biomass (Table 3, decarboxylic-acid transporter) x mu=0.05; 202*0.05=10.10. Cross-check: measured molar yield fumarate/acetate=2.8 x acetate 3.26=9.13 mmol/gDW/h

sec.method: specific flux 185 mmol/g biomass (Table 3) x mu=0.05; 185*0.05=9.25. Cross-check: measured molar yield succinate/acetate=2.9 x acetate 3.26=9.45

mfa.note: Table 3; fumarate uptake > succinate secretion indicates net fumarate consumption as C source
slightly >acetate uptake -> fumarate carbon enters TCA; Table 3
positive = gluconeogenesis initiation via OAA decarboxylation (dominant under fumarate); Table 3
net PEP/pyruvate -> acetyl-CoA (reversed vs Fe(III)); Table 3

growth_records.cite: Yang TH (Tae Hoon Yang) et al. Metabolic response of Geobacter sulfurreducens towards electron donor/acceptor variation. Microbial Cell Factories (2010). DOI:10.1186/1475-2859-9-90. [PMC3002917]
growth_records.snippet: the molar yield of succinate per mole of acetate consumed was 2.9 +/- 0.2 for E3 (5 mM acetate + 28 mM fumarate) ... The molar yield of fumarate uptake on acetate was 2.8 +/- 0.1 ... biomass yield coefficient ... 0.013 +/- 2 x 10-3 for E3
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state => mu=D=0.05/h (Table 1). td=ln2/0.05=13.86 h. Rates = Table 3 specific fluxes (mmol/g biomass) x mu. Measured molar yields (succinate/acetate 2.9, fumarate/acetate 2.8) give consistent cross-checks. Fumarate serves as electron acceptor AND additional carbon source (fumarate uptake 202 > succinate secretion 185 per g biomass). Under fumarate, gluconeogenesis initiates via PEPCK (OAA->PEP).

up.method: specific flux 66.3 mmol/g biomass (Table 3) x mu=D=0.05/h; 66.3*0.05=3.315
specific flux 177 mmol/g biomass (Table 3) x mu=0.05; 177*0.05=8.85. Cross-check: molar yield fumarate/acetate=2.5 x acetate 3.315=8.29 mmol/gDW/h

sec.method: specific flux 165 mmol/g biomass (Table 3) x mu=0.05; 165*0.05=8.25. Cross-check: molar yield succinate/acetate=2.5 x acetate 3.315=8.29

mfa.note: Table 3; fumarate uptake > succinate secretion -> net fumarate as C source
reductive branch; lower than E3; Table 3
~93% of PEP for gluconeogenesis from OAA; Table 3

growth_records.cite: Yang TH (Tae Hoon Yang) et al. Metabolic response of Geobacter sulfurreducens towards electron donor/acceptor variation. Microbial Cell Factories (2010). DOI:10.1186/1475-2859-9-90. [PMC3002917]
growth_records.snippet: the molar yield of succinate per mole of acetate consumed was ... 2.5 +/- 0.2 for E4 (10 mM acetate + 20 mM fumarate) ... The molar yield of fumarate uptake on acetate was ... 2.5 +/- 0.1 in ... E4 ... 0.012 +/- 2 x 10-3 for ... E4
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state => mu=D=0.05/h (Table 1). td=ln2/0.05=13.86 h. Rates = Table 3 specific fluxes (mmol/g biomass) x mu=0.05. Measured molar yields succinate/acetate=2.5 and fumarate/acetate=2.5 give consistent cross-checks. Acceptor-limited condition: ~93% of gluconeogenic PEP from OAA via PEPCK; reductive FRD5 flux (-99.5) lower than donor-limited E3 (-116).

up.units: mol per mol cellobiose (electron donor + biomass)

mfa.units: mol succinate per 2.23 mol fumarate per mol cellobiose
mfa.note: electron-acceptor limited; complete fumarate removal; succinate >10 mM inhibits C.c.

mfa.units: mol per mol cellobiose -> 0.6 mol CO2
mfa.note: electron donor; minor fraction to biomass

growth_records.cite: Miller, Lance D et al. Establishment and metabolic analysis of a model microbial community for understanding trophic and electron accepting interactions of subsurface anaerobic environments. BMC Microbiology (2010). DOI:10.1186/1471-2180-10-149. [PMC2906461]
growth_records.snippet: While 4.9 mM fumarate was provided to the tri-culture, 2.23 moles of fumarate were transformed per mole of cellobiose consumed. The 2.23 moles of fumarate were reduced to 1.63 moles of succinate with 0.02 moles of malate also detected
growth_records.notes: Minor fumarate-respiring member in anaerobic tri-culture chemostat. mu=D=0.03/h; doubling=23.1 h. Fumarate 4.9 mM completely consumed (measured): volumetric 0.03x4.9=0.147 mmol/L/h. Per mol cellobiose: 2.23 fumarate -> 1.63 succinate + 0.02 malate; ~0.45 acetate consumed, 0.3 oxidized -> 0.6 CO2 (Fig 5 model). Electron-acceptor limited.

derived.qs_mu_note: pooled across studies/media — not a single chemostat series, so no maintenance model fitted
